user_query:
I’m helping organize a small, in-person community movie night and I want something I can run offline on my home computer to review options with our planning group. Please set up a simple, local-only “Movie Night Catalog” from the provided files and deploy it as a static site I can open in my browser. Use only built-in tools (e.g., Python standard library) so nothing needs to be installed from the Internet.

Inputs (already in this workspace):
- input/movies.csv
- input/content_guidelines.html

What I need:
1) Extract structured information from both inputs:
   - Parse input/content_guidelines.html to capture the bullet points under the headings “Selection Criteria” and “Accessibility”. Treat these as the authoritative rules for evaluating movies.
   - Parse input/movies.csv into structured records.

2) Evaluate suitability by rules derived from the guidelines (do not hardcode different criteria):
   - If the guidelines say “Prefer MPAA rating G or PG.” then allowable ratings are {G, PG}.
   - If they say “Keep runtime under 120 minutes when possible.” please treat this as a threshold of <= 120 minutes for suitability.
   - If they say “Ensure captions or subtitles are available.” then captions must be available.
   Please apply these rules to every movie and compute:
   - suitable_per_guidelines: true/false
   - suitability_reasons: an array of short strings that explain which guideline each movie fails (or “meets all selection criteria”).

3) Write the following outputs under the specified paths:
   - out/catalog.json: a single JSON object with this structure:
     {
       "guidelines": {
         "selection_criteria": [list of strings extracted from the Selection Criteria bullets in content_guidelines.html],
         "accessibility": [list of strings extracted from the Accessibility bullets in content_guidelines.html]
       },
       "movies": [
         {
           "title": string,
           "year": integer,
           "mpaa_rating": string,
           "genre": string,
           "runtime_min": integer,
           "has_captions": boolean,
           "description": string,
           "short_note": first 12 words of description as a single string,
           "suitable_per_guidelines": boolean,
           "suitability_reasons": array of strings
         }, ...
       ]
     }
   - out/selection.csv: a CSV of only the suitable movies with header row and columns exactly in this order:
     title,year,mpaa_rating,runtime_min,has_captions,genre,short_note
   - out/site/index.html: a single static HTML page that can be opened locally. Requirements:
     - Include a visible summary element with id="summary-counts" whose text states: "Total movies: X; Suitable: Y" with the correct counts based on the data.
     - Include a simple list (ul/li) of the suitable movie titles.
     - Embed a script tag with type="application/json" and id="catalog-data" whose JSON content is byte-for-byte identical to out/catalog.json.
   - run_local.sh (Unix/Mac) and run_local.ps1 (Windows PowerShell) at the workspace root that each start a local static server from the out/site directory on http://localhost:8000 using Python’s built-in http.server. These should change directory to out/site and then launch the server.
   - out/meeting_notes.md: a concise planning note for our group with these sections and minimum content:
     - "Environment Setup": briefly state where the site lives (out/site), how to start it with the provided scripts, and where the data files are located.
     - "What Was Deployed": a sentence or two describing the static catalog and what it contains.
     - "Data Summary": include the total movie count, suitable count, and a bulleted list of unsuitable titles with a one-line reason (based on suitability_reasons).
     - "Action Items": at least four actionable bullets tailored from the extracted guidelines (e.g., testing offline playback on the community laptop, confirming captions, run-time considerations, preparing large-print summaries), plus one item to pick top 3 titles by a date placeholder.

Notes:
- Please make the extraction of guideline text from input/content_guidelines.html an essential step; use those exact bullets to drive the suitability check and to populate the “guidelines” section of out/catalog.json and to inform the Action Items in out/meeting_notes.md.
- Keep everything self-contained and offline. Do not fetch anything from the Internet.
- I will open out/site/index.html in my browser and use the run scripts if needed.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
